title: Board Advisor audit 2026-05-01 — repo hygiene, reboot-plan landing, T1-T5 parallel execution
description:
Source: Board Advisor audit conducted 2026-05-01 ~00:35 W. Europe at OWNER request, plus OWNER live observation that T1-T5 are not running in parallel. Owning role: CEO; decompose and assign as you see fit (do not pre-bake — spec-density principle).

## Context snapshot

- `public-data/company-runtime.json` overall_status = `alert`, autonomy = `monitor_only`.
- `git status` in main checkout shows 224 changed/untracked items.
- `runtime_health.detectors.stuck_session = 1` (Development).
- Weekly run count 14,639 → projected monthly 58,556 against a system that has not landed Phase 2 yet.

## Findings (8)

### F1 — Worktree discipline violated at scale (HIGH)
~120 `docs/ops/QUA-*_*.{md,json}` artifacts and 30+ `QUA-346_*` files are untracked in main checkout. Per the established rule (per-agent worktrees write to their own branch only), Doc-KM-class artifacts belong in `agents/docs-km` worktree, not main. Source-of-truth files also modified-but-uncommitted in main: `CLAUDE.md`, `framework/V5_FRAMEWORK_DESIGN.md`, `framework/registry/{ea_id_registry,magic_numbers}.csv`, `framework/include/QM/QM_*.mqh`. This is the V4 mass-delete failure class (DL-028 isolation). Suggested owner: Documentation-KM + DevOps (sweep + a pre-commit/scheduled enforcer).

### F2 — Garbage files at repo root (LOW, but signal of nobody-reads-diff)
Four 0-byte files created 2026-04-27 14:46 from a misquoted shell command, sitting in `C:/QM/repo/`: `Append-only`, `Authoritative`, `agents,`, `hourly-public`. Suggested owner: DevOps (delete + add a top-level untracked-file guard).

### F3 — `decisions/DL-029_strategy_research_workflow.md` deletion in limbo (MEDIUM)
Status `D`, uncommitted, no successor visible in `decisions/`. Either commit deletion + successor in one commit, or `git restore` it. Suggested owner: Documentation-KM with Research Lead consult.

### F4 — `PROJECT_BACKLOG.md` is materially out of date (HIGH)
`PROJECT_BACKLOG.md` § Today's Reality still says "Paperclip not installed yet, only OWNER and Board Advisor are active." Runtime shows 14 agents, CEO doing 12 runs/hour, CTO 32 runs/hour, Documentation-KM 7 runs/hour. The doc agents are supposed to read first lies about today's state. Suggested owner: Documentation-KM (rewrite Today's Reality table + Phase 1 partial-done markers, link reboot plan as live initiative).

### F5 — 2026-04-30 reboot plan never executed (HIGH)
`docs/ops/PAPERCLIP_COMPANY_REBOOT_PLAN_2026-04-30.md` and the matching `_ISSUES_2026-04-30.md` are untracked. None of the 7 packet issues (Chief of Staff, org/token review, Video Researcher YouTube task, T1-T5 DWX audit, Company Operating Model dashboard, lessons-loop process update, Gmail intake feasibility) appear in the runtime. Plan sat ~30h while system burned ~2k runs/day in old shape. Decision needed: execute or shelve. Suggested owner: CEO direct (this is a CEO-level decision, not delegable).

### F6 — Token burn vs throughput inverted (HIGH)
14,639 runs/week with no V5 EA through P10. Specific bleeders:
- `QUA-211` open: Pipeline-Operator process_loss failure pattern, 18% fail rate over 24h (medium).
- `QUA-372` open (high): Development hot-poll loop produced 100 identical heartbeat comments on QUA-302.
- 67 blocked issues; 16 assigned to CEO, 12 to Development, 11 to CTO, 7 to Pipeline-Operator.
Suggested owner: CEO triage; CTO closes QUA-372 root cause first (hot-poll is recursive cost amplifier).

### F7 — Research stall + Strategy Card jam (MEDIUM)
Research agent last heartbeat 2026-04-28T22:53 (~50h silent, but `wake_on_demand=true`). 10 SRC04 Lien cards (QUA-316/318/319/323/324/326/...) blocked, no assignee. SRC01 Davey cards (QUA-277/278/281) blocked on CEO/CTO. The bottleneck is build-queue throughput, not card supply. Adding sources before clearing the build queue is waste. Suggested owner: CEO (decision: pause Research vs unblock build queue first).

### F8 — T1-T5 are not running in parallel (HIGH — OWNER live observation)
Process 16 Rule 3 (binding, ratified under DL-038) codifies: "All five factory terminals (T1-T5) participate concurrently in dispatch. The 15-job concurrent ceiling (3 active jobs per terminal × 5 terminals) is the default; T3, T4, T5 must not sit idle when work is available." OWNER reports this is not happening (2026-05-01). Existing tracking: QUA-413 (DevOps deploy) and QUA-414 (matrix dispatcher) — both transitioned but symptom persists. Need: verify `D:\QM\Reports\pipeline\dispatch_state.json` running counts per terminal during a live dispatch; if T3-T5 idle while queue non-empty, that's a Rule 3 violation and a dispatcher anomaly per the rule's own acceptance signal. Speed bottleneck = strategy-throughput bottleneck = the entire Phase 3 timeline. Suggested owner: Pipeline-Operator (dispatch evidence) + DevOps (EA fan-out prerequisite per Rule 5) + CTO (review).

## Acceptance gate (CEO defines exact criteria; minimum bar)

- F1, F2, F3 closed with commits in correct worktrees + proof.
- F4 backlog Today's Reality table reflects 2026-05-01 truth.
- F5 explicit GO/NO-GO decision recorded in a decision file.
- F6 QUA-211 + QUA-372 root cause closed; weekly run rate trending down or justified.
- F7 explicit decision on Research pause vs queue unblock.
- F8 dispatch_state.json evidence captured during a real run showing all 5 terminals at running_count > 0 when queue depth permits.

## Forbidden

- Do not let this issue become a 50-comment heartbeat log. Decompose into sub-issues per finding and resolve there.
- Do not violate worktree discipline while fixing F1 — double the violation.
- Do not touch T6 in any sub-fix.
